wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/barnet-vale (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt: Mukesh is a resident of the ward and wants to make it a better place to live, work and play for all. He has over 20 years' experience of designing and delivering service improvements to a range of public, private and community organisations. This includes local councils across London, enabling them to deliver better services and achieve value for money for residents and businesses.

Mark Francis has lived in Barnet for many years.
He has spent most of his working life fixing things — running hands-on businesses in car repairs, landscaping, building, property maintenance and development.
Mark is a problem-solver who believes in getting results, not making excuses. He knows how to identify what's broken and put it right.
Barnet now faces serious challenges.
It's time to fix it.
Previously, I was Councillor for High Barnet. I live in Barnet Vale ward, and hope to be re-elected on May 7th to serve my local community and stand up for local residents with problems.

Uri Mofsowitz is an entrepreneur and business leader who built a successful company in Cape Town. Six years ago, he moved to the UK to create a better future for his family. Since settling in Barnet Vale, he has become deeply committed to the area and its community.
Uri believes residents deserve clean, safe neighbourhoods, responsible local spending, and a council that listens to local people. Concerned by the decline he has witnessed, Uri is standing to bring practical business thinking, accountability, and a strong voice for residents at Barnet Council.

Born in Barnet, I am a director of a local landscape architecture consultancy and project manager. A local campaigner I founded the Friends of Tudor Park & Pavilion and I’m the Chair of the registered charity. My main objective if elected is to ensure the Tudor Park pavilion is refurbished and reused for the benefit of the local community. I also priorities local environmental and sustainable issues in planning developments & healthy streets.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/brunswick-park (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
I was born in London and have spent much of my life serving the Barnet community.

My professional career has been in banking and litigation, giving me experience in dealing with complex issues and standing up for people when it matters most.

I served as councillor for Brunswick Park Ward for 16 years from 2006 and was honoured to be elected Mayor of Barnet in 2012. During my time in office, I successfully campaigned for the extension of the 383 bus route to Finchley Memorial Hospital, improving access for patients and residents.

I also helped create the Essential Care Giver policy through Parliament to support people living with dementia. I founded the charity Dementia Club UK, which supports people living with dementia and their families across the community.

I am known for being reliable, determined and never afraid to stand up for residents.

Chris Johnstone has lived in Barnet for 50 years and has deep roots in the local community. Over that time, he has seen the area change and understands both the strengths of Barnet and the challenges residents face today. His children were educated locally, giving him a long-standing connection to local schools and neighbourhoods.
Chris is a retired Chartered Civil Engineer and also holds an MBA in IT Management. During his professional career he gained experience managing projects, budgets and technical teams, giving him a strong understanding of how organisations should operate efficiently and responsibly. With the time and experience to serve residents Chris wants to focus on delivering better value for money across council services, particularly when it comes to contracted work. If elected Chris will be approachable, responsive, and committed to listening to residents and helping resolve local issues.

I was born and raised in Barnet and have a strong connection to the local community. Having grown up in the area I understand the everyday challenges residents face and the importance of keeping Barnet a safe and supportive place for families and local neighbourhoods across the borough. I work as a self-employed electrician and brings hard work, reliability and practical problem solving to everything I do, qualities that are equally important when representing residents and addressing local issues.
I'm also a father of a young boy at a local primary school, giving me a personal interest in improving local schools, safer streets and stronger support for working families in the community.
If elected I will be a visible and accessible councillor who listens to residents, represents their concerns and works hard to ensure their interests always come first.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/burnt-oak (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
I live locally, my daughter went to school in the area and I use the same community services you do.

I have a strong track record of advocating for workers' rights in my career and for fair outcomes for residents locally, particularly in relation to housing.

I am Gary Gadston, a resident of Edgware with social and business roots in the area dating back to the Sixties.

Having run my own construction business for over 40 years, I have experienced how wasteful spending, poor decisions, and excessive bureaucracy from local authorities drive up costs, placing severe and unnecessary financial pressure on small businesses and working families.

I believe Barnet Council has lost control of its finances. Residents are paying the price through ever-increasing Council debt and tax bills. The introduction of transparent financial disciplines need to be introduced, with a clear focus on fairness for residents rather than bureaucracy.

I am committed to protecting the residents and the character of Burnt Oak, and neighboring communities by advocating for sensible development that provides appropriate infrastructure and improvements to local services. Overdevelopment without proper planning must be challenged.

I’m absolutely committed to Burnt Oak. Since moving to the area in 2022, I have been extremely proactive in tackling some of deeply entrenched issues facing this neglected, and for too long overlooked, part of Barnet borough.  In the short number of years I have been here have founded a Friends of Group for Watling Park, have pioneered a community garden to tackle a substantial flytipping hotspot, and am taking action on reducing anti-social behaviour in our ward.

I am recognised by people in our community as somebody who takes action to better our area. Every day I step out of my door, I take action for the neighbourhood - be that reporting fly-tipping, supporting a small business over a high-street chain, or picking up litter from our streets.

I want to celebrate Burnt Oak as a fantastic place to live - I am galvanised by the pride/ community spirit in Burnt Oak, comprised of people who care about our neighbourhood but who are frustrated by issues such as anti-social behaviour, drug/alcohol abuse in public spaces, and fly-tipping, and their concerns going repeatedly unheard e.g the proposed developments to build on a floodplain on the Watling Car Park site; wasting much-needed Council funds (and going against their commitment to net Zero) by building a new Library rather than re-vamping the existing one.

I’m a recognised face within Burnt Oak as someone who works hard to improve the area- residents have already started coming to me to help represent their views on/ oppose issues such as inappropriate planning, developing community green spaces, and tackling crime.

I would be an effective, competent Green councillor who would inspire confidence due to the results I have already delivered as a resident, coupled with my knowledge of activities/issues/initiatives/networks/key stakeholders locally.   Burnt Oak is a prime location to benefit from the implementation/realisation of core Green policies/values e.g taking a public health view to poverty, investing in qua

I’m Tony Mace and have lived in Burnt Oak all my life. I’ve watched our area deteriorate — rising anti-social behaviour, constant fly-tipping, everyday shoplifting, endless roadworks and unfair parking charges dumped on residents without proper consultation. This isn’t how Burnt Oak should be.

I’ve followed Nigel Farage’s political career since 2014, and Reform UK is the only party offering the common-sense solutions we desperately need: real deterrents against crime, value for money, and proper accountability from Barnet Council.

I’ve worked my way up from being an apprentice engineer in the railway industry to full-time employment, and I know what ordinary working people need from their local area: safety, cleanliness, and services that actually work.

I’m standing because Burnt Oak needs fresh leadership — and someone prepared to speak up for what local residents are really experiencing and need.

Vote for me to give Burnt Oak the care, pride and common sense it deserves.

I'm Oliver Segal — born and raised in Barnet, now working in public service.
If elected for Burnt Oak, I will put residents first and bring real change. With a background in physics and experience in public-sector administration, I know how to fix broken systems and end years of neglect. Burnt Oak deserves better — and I'm ready to deliver it.

Having lived in the ward for the last nine years, I am deeply committed to our community and the people who live here. I am standing as a Liberal Democrat candidate to bring a combination of experienced leadership, professional expertise, and a proven track record of public service to our local council.
**A Record of Community Service**
My commitment to young people and community cohesion is central to my life. I am currently a volunteer Scout Leader at the 2nd Kingsbury Scout Group. Previously, I served as the Scout District Commissioner for Brent from 2017 to 2025. During my eight-year tenure, I had the privilege of leading what became the fastest-growing and most diverse Scout district in the United Kingdom, ensuring that young people from all backgrounds had access to skills for life.
**Professional Experience and Leadership**
In my professional life, I work as a Software Solution Architect, specialising in designing complex systems for cruise ships. This role requires rigorous problem-solving, long-term strategic planning, and the ability to deliver results within budget.
I am a Chartered IT Professional and a Member of the British Computer Society (MBCS). Furthermore, as a Member of The Institute of Leadership and an Associate of King's College London (AKC), I bring a disciplined and ethical approach to leadership that I believe is essential for effective local government.
**Proven Political Experience**
I have a long-standing history of representing residents in local government. I served as a District Councillor in West Lindsey (2003–2008) and as a Town Councillor for Market Rasen (2002–2010). Between 2004 and 2006, I served as the Town Mayor of Market Rasen; at the time, I held the record as the youngest ever Mayor in the UK.
This experience taught me how to navigate the complexities of local authorities to get things done for residents. I understand how councils work, how to advocate for better services, and how to ensure that your voice is heard at every

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/childs-hill (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt: I am an accomplished financial services entrepreneur who read Philosophy, Politics and Economics at Oxford and currently lead a local company focused on financial inclusion. Born in London, I'm a proud Childs Hill resident who is passionate about community facilities and reversing the decline of local high streets. Father to two daughters, I'm standing to ensure Childs Hill benefits from a common-sense approach to law and order bringing safety back to our streets.

My name is Jake Videtzky and I'm running for the Green Party in Barnet because I believe it's brand of a politics of hope. There is an overwhelming feeling of pessimism in my generation, which I hear in conversations ranging from the job market to geopolitics. Yet in the face in these sentiments the Green Party has offered us unity in the face of division and an optimism grounded in the potential for change.

I’ve lived in the Golders Green and Childs Hill area for several years, and I understand the day-to-day pressures residents face — from rising council tax and housing demand to concerns about how well local services are actually delivered.

I’ve worked with children and young people across the borough, and I care deeply about making Childs Hill a place where families feel safe, streets are well maintained, and local services are reliable.

Too often, residents feel the council isn’t focused enough on results. I believe councillors should be judged on simple things: are services improving, is money being spent wisely, and are residents being listened to? That includes cleaner streets, better maintained estates, and making sure residents actually see value from what they pay in council tax.

I strongly support protecting and improving local libraries, which remain vital community spaces for people of all ages.

I studied Ancient History at the University of St Andrews, where I learned how political systems succeed or fail based on the decisions they make. That perspective shapes my approach: practical, careful, and focused on what actually works.

I’m standing to give Childs Hill a clear, accountable voice on the council — focused on delivering better services, sensible spending, and consistent attention to local concerns.

I have worked across London as a carpenter, in music industry logistics and promotions, and later in mental health after studying psychology at Middlesex University.
Having witnessed political change from childhood through adulthood, I understand both the progress and mistakes made by past governments.
Once politically disillusioned, I believe Reform UK now offers a genuine voice for ordinary people. I am standing in Childs Hill to challenge establishment politics, listen to residents, and help deliver practical, positive change for the community.

We are campaigning to bring back Liberal Democrat councillors to Mill Hill. Councillors who will put the needs of residents first, if elected on May 5th our promise to you is that we will implement the following: 1: Have your voice heard by the Council 2: Bring back street skip collections to end the plague of fly tipping 3: We will cancel the current attempts to run commercial events in our parks all year round 4: We will protect your interests and our much needed Green Belt.

candidates.stmt:
Originally from Iran, Reza has lived and worked across London for 26 years and has been a long-standing Edgware resident.
A successful Petroleum and Gas Engineer with two Master's degrees in Petroleum Engineering and Environmental Remote Sensing, he brings strong technical and professional experience.
Reza is passionate about restoring pride in Barnet, having witnessed the decline of local services and community trust. He believes residents deserve to be heard and represented properly.
Standing for election for the first time, he is committed to accountability, common-sense policies and responsible spending.
Copy ward link

Paolo Negretti is a financial trader with international experience across finance, housing and public policy. He believes Britain needs genuine reform from the ground up — cutting red tape, challenging complacency and focusing on practical solutions that improve everyday life.
Paolo wants to bring his professional expertise and hands-on approach to Colindale North, standing up for residents, strengthening local services and ensuring the council delivers real value for taxpayers.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/colindale-south (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
Susanne Hannington is an experienced finance and operations professional with over 40 years' expertise across the cycling industry, banking and community leadership. She has managed high-value financial operations, overseeing payment collections in the UK and internationally.
Now semi-retired, Susanne supports youth development through work at a Barnet primary school and as Treasurer and Director of a local football club serving over 700 children. She wants to boost Colindale South and be a strong local voice.

Marek Vojčík is a Slovak immigrant and NHS mental-health nurse for over 20 years, he is not a career politician but a people-focused professional who believes every penny of council tax should be spent wisely.

Marek's priorities are care, honesty and fairness. He is committed to making Colindale safer, cleaner and better, and to securing a stronger future for local families and children.

Mohsen Ojany was born in Iran and moved to the UK to build a life, career and future, living in Colindale since 2008. A Business Management and Marketing graduate from Middlesex University, he has experience in family-run beauty and hospitality businesses and over a decade in the property sector.
As a homeowner and long-term resident, Mohsen understands Colindale's growth and challenges and is committed to practical, accountable leadership that improves residents' quality of life.

We listen to local people. 
In just a few weeks’ time, residents in Barnet will have the opportunity to elect a new Council.Your local Liberal Democrat team in Barnet have been busy sending surveys to over 6,000 local residents to understand your priorities for Barnet Council
Based on our conversations with thousands of local residents, we are proud to announce the priorities that we will be fighting for at these elections:
* Tackling crime and helping people to feel safe in their neighbourhood by campaigning for more community police officers and fighting cuts to the Safer Neighbourhood Teams.
* Fighting fly-tipping and litter by streamlining and monitoring the ways to report antisocial behaviour in Barnet.
* Addressing issues with traffic management by consulting with local people about parking and speed restrictions, rather than using fines as a way to balance the budget. 
* Campaigning to reduce utility bills, petitioning the government to do more to help families struggling with the Cost of Living.
We are a party of action. 
The Barnet Lib Dems are part of the London Lib Dem family. We have six Liberal Democrat Members of Parliament.
Luke Taylor MP (Spokesperson for London) has championed a new approach to tackling knife crime as a public health issue. Sarah Olney MP has campaigned for tougher action against Thames Water’s failures. Bobby Dean MP has been fighting for better NHS provision. Paul Kohler MP is campaigning for Restorative Justice, to put victims at the heart of the criminal justice system. And as education spokesperson Munira Wilson MP has been campaigning for better services for children with special educational needs. Ed Davey MP is the Leader of the Liberal Democrats, he champions liberal values at all levels, holding the government to account, challenging Donald Trump and Nigel Farage, whilst also supporting local residents in his Kingston constituency.
We care about you, your family and your neighbours.
From setting up community gardens, volun

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/cricklewood (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
I have lived in Barnet all my life and understand the issues facing local residents.

After more than 25 years building a successful career in the City, I now also care for my elderly mother, giving me first-hand experience of the pressures so many families face.

I am a volunteer with the Royal Free Trust, supporting a new initiative to improve the experience of patients and their families. I have also successfully campaigned against misguided proposals from Barnet Council.

I will bring professional integrity and a practical, common-sense approach to problem-solving if elected — focused on improving the everyday lives of local people.

Jamie Dawood works in the City of London, with degrees in Chemical Engineering and Management, and has lived in Barnet for over twenty years. Like many local residents, he has seen firsthand how poor decision-making and complacency at the council have led to rising costs and declining services. Families pay more every year yet receive less in return.
Jamie is standing for election because Barnet deserves a financially responsible, transparent council focused on delivering quality services. He will bring a disciplined, practical approach, restore trust, balance the books, and ensure future generations are not left paying for today's mistakes.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/east-barnet (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
As a lifelong resident of East Barnet Ward, I have a deep connection to the local community and a clear understanding of both its strengths and the challenges it faces. I care passionately about protecting what makes this area such a great place to live and raising the services of the Council to a standard that local residents deserve.

I am standing to protect what matters most to local people: keeping our area safe, pleasant and affordable. My priorities are clear: resisting rising taxes, protecting weekly bin collections, tackling potholes, improving street safety and attracting quality shops to our high streets.

These objectives are increasingly at risk due to increasing budget shortfalls facing Barnet Council, year-on-year. We need leadership that makes responsible decisions and focuses on delivering for residents.

That is why I am proud to stand for Reform, a party committed to cutting waste, governing with efficiency and putting local residents first.

Karl Khan has spent more than 20 years owning and running a successful public house, giving him deep, hands-on experience in the hospitality sector and a real understanding of the pressures facing local businesses and working families. Alongside this, he has worked extensively in both the transport and hospitality industries, building a reputation for reliability, hard work and community engagement.
Having previously stood in Barnet elections, Karl knows the borough well and is determined to speak up for residents who feel ignored by the main parties. He supports Nigel Farage's common-sense policies and wants to bring practical, real-world leadership to East Barnet—cutting waste, improving local services, and standing up for ordinary people.

Dima Ouda is a highly experienced former BBC Senior Journalist, with years of work covering major national and international events, holding power to account, and giving a voice to people whose stories needed to be told. She came to the UK under the High-Skilled Visa Programme 23 years ago, and since then she has worked hard, contributed, and paid her taxes without ever relying on benefits. Having built her life here, Dima cares deeply about this country and its future.

She believes that illegal immigration poses significant risks to the UK’s economy, welfare system, and national security, and that these issues are too often overlooked. Dima is also deeply concerned about the danger of violent Islamic extremism, having personally witnessed her country of origin fall to it. With the UK now her second home, she is determined to ensure it never faces the same fate. She believes strongly that Reform UK is the only party willing to confront these issues honestly and defend the values that keep Britain safe and stable.

⸻

Why She Wants to Stand as a Council Candidate

Dima wants to stand because she strongly believes in the party’s values of security, integrity, and restoring pride in Britain. Having lived in the UK for over 23 years — and having seen first-hand, through her work at the BBC, how national issues affect everyday communities — she understands the challenges the country is facing. Dima believes she can contribute meaningfully to Reform’s goals by helping deliver safer streets, responsible governance, and a council that stands up for residents’ priorities. Her commitment to the UK’s safety, prosperity and long-term future is why Reform UK’s vision aligns so closely with her own.

Dear Resident,

Like many local residents I have become pretty disillusioned with the state of politics in Britain. But on Thursday 7th May 2026 voters in the local elections across the London Borough of Barnet and right across the country will have the opportunity of having their say in the local elections. No doubt the national picture will influence the outcome.

This year there will be a record number of candidates standing across the borough. It is nevertheless critical that people come out and cast their votes. I am standing as a Liberal Democrat for the East Barnet area.

As a resident living in the East Barnet Ward - one of the few (if any) within the boundary - and for over 25 years now, I have campaigned on various issues over the years with a track record of success. I passionately care about the area and my mission has involved amongst other things:

*	Campaigning to save The Prince of Wales (‘PoW’) pub in the heart of East Barnet.
*	Involved with the ‘Save New Barnet’ campaign over proposed ASDA/Tesco development with letters, PR and BBC TV coverage. Various other letters to press over library cuts.
*	Liaising for around 2 years with Stonegate Group (PoW’s ex-owner) to get a dangerous tree cut back and lopped at bottom of Jackson Road, which had damaged cars. No cost to residents.
*    	Raising over £500 for gold leaf re-gilding of WW2 names on a War Memorial in the area.
* 	Managing to get several local residents off car parking fines. Street and alley cleaning efforts.

In the event I am elected for this ward I will continue my efforts as described above. Furthermore I will campaign and stand up for small businesses in the area, charities, those facing cost-of-living pressures and the environment around Green-flag status Oakhill Park.

Yours Sincerely,

Roger L. Aitken

**

Note: Promoted by Sean Hooker, Liberal Democrats, on behalf of RL Aitken, both of 5 Ibsley Way, Barnet, EN4 9EY.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/east-finchley (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt: I’ve lived in East Finchley for 24 years, and I’ve seen what makes it such a diverse, caring and welcoming community. Through my work as a Food Bank volunteer and primary school reading assistant, I’m constantly inspired by the generosity of local people, and I want to bring that same energy to serving residents as your Green councillor. I’ll work to make our streets safer and cleaner, ensure new homes are genuinely affordable and right for our area, and put community wellbeing at the heart of every decision.

I’m standing as a Green candidate because I believe our future depends on the choices we make today. My experience at the University of Essex Students’ Union, representing over 14,000 students, showed me how important it is to turn people’s concerns into real action by improving affordability, access and everyday services.

It reinforced my belief that we need to move away from short-term thinking and build a system that prioritises sustainability, stronger communities and long-term wellbeing.

I’m standing to help build a fairer, greener future that genuinely works for everyone.

I’m proud to be standing for the Green Party in East Finchley. Our two-party system has failed too many for too long, preventing residents from having a voice that is truly theirs in the council chamber. It’s time for the old parties to stop taking our votes for granted and for a council that does things differently. Vote Green on May 7th!

Daniel Brindusescu has over 10 years' experience in the security industry, working with diverse communities. Having lived in Barnet for more than seven years, he understands how vital effective council services are to local wellbeing.
With academic qualifications in leadership, human resources, and internal audit, he brings strong values of governance and accountability.
Daniel believes Barnet has been failed by Labour and the Conservatives and supports Reform UK's focus on efficiency, transparency, and putting residents first.

Mel Haskins has lived in Barnet for many years and is keen to give back to the community he has called home for more than half his life. With over 45 years’ experience in international banking technology, Mel built a distinguished career leading major financial software projects and advising global institutions including IBM and JP Morgan. He previously served as Managing Director of BIS Banking Systems and later founded his own consultancy, working with banks and technology firms worldwide.
A long-time supporter of Brexit, Mel became disillusioned with the way the Conservatives handled its delivery. He believes Reform UK offers a fresh direction, with practical policies to control illegal immigration, reduce bureaucracy and get Britain working again.

Paresh Kiri – Building Stronger Lives and a Better East Finchley
Paresh Kiri believes that when people operate in alignment with their strengths and values, performance and success follow naturally. This principle has shaped both his career and his approach to leadership and coaching.
With a background as a Director and Private Client Discretionary Manager in financial services, Paresh has spent years managing complex portfolios, navigating risk, and building trusted relationships across diverse sectors. His ability to bring clarity and alignment where others see complexity has been a defining strength.
He has also served as MC at prestigious events, including at the House of Lords hosted by Lord and Lady Loomba, and at the Institute of Directors, where he has supported initiatives championing Women of Influence and modern leadership qualities such as clarity, resilience, and authenticity.
Alongside his professional work, Paresh is developing an elite coaching practice focused on helping individuals and leaders improve performance through better energy management, clearer purpose, and stronger personal foundations. His approach, influenced by his work within the TKC Leadership ecosystem under Kirit Pankhania, blends leadership, wellbeing, and practical results — without losing sight of real-world outcomes.
His expertise includes financial services, portfolio management, risk and trading, property, business development, and leadership coaching.
Paresh is also committed to making East Finchley a better place to live — supporting safer streets, stronger local businesses, and a real sense of community. He believes local representatives should be visible, accountable, and focused on delivering practical improvements that residents can see and feel.

Julia has been a volunteer in East Finchley for years, as a board member of Grange Big Local, a trustee of Barnet Lone Parent Centre, a volunteer at Finchley Toy Library and a vaccine centre volunteer.  Her interest in how the council works began when she chaired Age UK Barnet. Her community work means she has had hundreds of conversations with people in East Finchley.  Professionally, she has worked as both a doctor and a solicitor.  She thinks the area needs a fresh voice and fresh ideas and supports the green, clean, healthy and safe manifesto of Barnet Lib Dems.

I live on The Grange estate in East Finchley where I have fought to protect vital green spaces in the area from overdevelopment. The disruption and anger caused by the poorly-planned A1000 cycle lane make me determined that we build a cycle network across the area that allows all road users to coexist in harmony. In my day job I advise public-sector organisations on strategy. I'm a keen runner, cyclist and dog-owner. I want to ensure that everyone feels safe on their doorstep, to promote green infrastructure, and to preserve what makes the area in special against the threat of over-development.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/edgware (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
Anita Dunner lives in Edgware and is standing for the first time as a local election candidate. She has grown to love the area while recognising where the council has fallen short. She believes residents deserve a stronger voice in local decisions.
Anita has been a teacher for the last 20 years.
Concerned about parking, road and pavement conditions, access to GP and dental services, and cleaner, safer streets, Anita wants a transparent council focused on practical improvements. If elected, she will challenge wasteful spending and deliver fair, common-sense solutions for Barnet residents.

Derek French has lived in Edgware Ward since 1976 and brings decades of experience in banking, consultancy and aviation. He holds an MBA from Middlesex University and has over 40 years’ military service as both a regular and reservist. Derek has seen significant changes in Edgware and strongly opposes overdevelopment, including the Ballymore Broadwalk scheme backed by Conservatives and approved by Labour. He will challenge wasteful spending, cut unnecessary bureaucracy, and ensure council decisions reflect residents’ priorities and protect the character of Edgware.
Copy ward link

Michael Sims is a senior business and technology leader with over 35 years' in business delivering complex projects and managing engineering teams across the world. A former CEO and technical director, he has worked with companies such as: GE, HP and Qualcomm , and advised the Mayor of London on Smart City Project. Michael is standing for Reform UK to cut waste, fix inefficiency, and ensure local services deliver real value for taxpayers.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/edgwarebury (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
Barak Mordechai Seener is an international security specialist with senior experience in counter-terrorism, geopolitical risk and financial crime. He has advised governments, banks, thinktanks, defence institutions and major organisations, lectured at NATO and leading defence colleges, published widely, and provided expert commentary for major global broadcasters.
Barak believes illegal immigration, rising extremism, failing public services and spiralling living costs are placing unsustainable pressure on Britain. He is a strong supporter of Israel and of tackling antisemitism at its root, to protect communities here in the UK.
Now turning his focus locally, Barak is standing with Reform UK to restore security, accountability and common-sense leadership where it matters most.

I am a lifelong North London resident, having grown up in East Finchley, and I have chosen to raise my family locally. As a mother of three, I understand the realities of family life and the importance of strong local services.

I bring nearly 20 years’ experience as a teacher, which has given me a deep commitment to education and to supporting pupils, schools and families. I am a passionate advocate for green spaces, wildlife and animal welfare, and I am determined to protect our parks and ensure North London and Edgwarebury remain clean, safe and thriving places for all generations.

I have lived in Barnet my whole life, growing up in Hendon and going to school in Edgwarebury. I've returned home after uni because I'm proud of where I grew up and I know this is a great place to live.

I have worked to support the Barnet-based charity ADDISS for over 6 years, including as a mentor for boys with ADHD at Hasmonean. I have also worked for a large charity, MP, and trade union, specialising in communications & democracy. My career has given me a strong understanding of our community, how to get things done, and the importance of good local representatives.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/finchley-church-end (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt: I was raised in a family of service, instilled with respect for duty and country. My father was a RAF Wing Commander and met my mother during the war when she served as WRAF Pilot Officer. I chose law over the military, qualifying as a solicitor in 1980 and co-founding a successful firm in 1983. I have been a resident of Barnet since 1979 but my wife has lived in Barnet for 65 years. I have also served as Honorary Consul and Trade Commissioner to the West African Republics of Sierra Leone and Liberia, involved in complex pre and post – war initiatives. I am standing for Reform UK. and  offer experience, independent and diligent year round representation for the residents of Finchley Church End.

Spencer Landeg is a lifelong Church End, Finchley resident educated locally and trained through a Ministry of Defence apprenticeship at Mill Hill Barracks.
He later founded a small business in Finchley, becoming Vice Chair of the local Chamber of Commerce, and served many years in the Army Reserve, including full-time service in Barnet.
With deep local knowledge, Spencer believes Barnet can deliver better services, tackle crime and antisocial behaviour, cut waste, and give real value for money to residents and businesses.

My name is Paolo Emilio Zanini, and I am standing as your candidate for Finchley Church End to provide the active, visible leadership our community deserves. I am committed to securing Freedom for our drivers by introducing 15 minutes of free parking to boost our high streets. I will demand Accountability from both the Council and developers to stop "tower block" over-development that ignores our local character and strains our GPs and schools. My plan puts our Community first by restoring green spaces like the Dollis Brook Valley and ensuring faster repairs for our roads. I will fight for total Transparency, providing a line-by-line breakdown of how your Council Tax is spent to end wasteful spending. Finally, I will prioritize the Security of our families by tackling anti-social behavior and increasing police presence in key areas like Finchley Central Station. Finchley deserves a representative who puts people before politics and delivers real results. On Election Day, make your voice count.

candidates.stmt:
I am standing to represent Friern Barnet ward because I believe local people should have a real voice in the decisions that shape their community.

I was born in Barnet and have lived in Friern Barnet for over 15 years. I am a teacher at a local secondary school, with experience of advocating for my fellow workers and the wider community as a trade union representative. As your councillor, I would bring that experience to support residents, work to protect our green spaces, and fight for the changes we all deserve: truly affordable housing, an end to austerity in local government, a social care system that meets people’s needs, and cleaner, safer streets for everyone.

On May 7th, vote for **Cameron Camiña**, **Stephen Last** and **Linda Lusingu** in **Friern Barnet**, because the **Green Party** are the only party offering real change, in Friern Barnet and beyond.

As we approach the local election on May 7th, we want to let residents know what it means to vote Green:

**OUR VALUES:**
✅Hope over hate   
✅Truth and transparency
✅People over profit 
✅A sustainable world

**OUR PRIORITIES IN BARNET:**
✅Pushing for investment in public services and prioritising well-being 
✅Holding the council to account and demanding transparency
✅Fighting for more affordable, energy-efficient and accessible homes
✅Ensuring streets and green spaces are safer and cleaner
✅Strengthening communities across Barnet

**WHY YOU SHOULD VOTE GREEN:**
✅We are local people **working hard** on behalf of local people
✅We will bring **fresh energy and ideas** to a tired council
✅We will unite communities with **a message of hope**

Labour and Conservatives have broken promises and failed residents. Their only plan is more austerity, more cuts, and more poverty.  We invite everyone in Barnet to join the campaign and help turn the council Green. Your voice matters, and together, we can create real change.

Fancy getting involved? https://join.greenparty.org.uk/

It’s been a real privilege to serve you as your councillor here in Friern Barnet since 2022. I have stood up for you in the council chamber, raising your concerns, fighting for your priorities, and making sure your voice is heard, and I am ready to keep going. Barnet is vibrant, Barnet is diverse, and that diversity deserves to be represented where decisions are made. I am standing as a Green Party candidate because the party’s values align with mine: fairness, hope, and putting people first. And importantly, there is no party whip, so I won’t be told how to vote. Every decision I make will be guided by you, the residents.

On May 7th, vote for **Cameron Camiña**, **Stephen Last** and **Linda Lusingu** in **Friern Barnet**, because the **Green Party** are the only party offering real change, in Friern Barnet and beyond.

I am standing to represent my fellow residents in Friern Barnet because they deserve better than business as usual, and I intend to deliver on the issues that matter to them.

I have lived in Friern Barnet for a long time, so I know how great this area can be and it's our residents who make it great. I want to give locals the representation they deserve and as your councillor I would bring a wealth of experience from the success I've achieved in my professional career. I am a Chartered Environmentalist who has helped large charities and successful British businesses by saving money through improving efficiency, reducing their environmental impact by operating responsibly, and delivering on their organisational objectives by meeting the needs of customers. If elected, I would bring fresh energy and ideas to address local issues, enhance green spaces, strengthen our communities, and listen to all residents as their voice in Barnet.

On May 7th, vote for **Cameron Camiña**, **Stephen Last** and **Linda Lusingu** in **Friern Barnet**, because the **Green Party** are the only party offering real change, in Friern Barnet and beyond.

Having resided in Barnet for around 50 years, I have deep roots in the area. My children and grandchildren have all attended local schools, giving me a strong personal connection to the community and its future. My professional career spans sales and marketing, followed by many years as a mortgage broker and financial adviser. Alongside this, I served nearly 20 years as a frontline volunteer police officer in Barnet, working directly to keep local residents safe. Now retired, I am able to dedicate more time to serving the community I have lived in for most of my life.

Joseph Pearce grew up in North London and has lived here for most of his life. He is an active member of Emmanuel North London Church and values the respect, community spirit, and traditions that have shaped Britain over centuries. Concerned about the decline he has seen locally, Joseph wants to help restore pride in our borough.
An IT professional with an understanding of business, he recognises the challenges facing local companies and high streets. His priorities include maintaining roads, improving traffic flow, protecting green spaces, planting more trees, and keeping streets clean. Joseph also believes strongly in public safety and open, respectful dialogue within the community

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/garden-suburb (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
William Comet has worked as a business and personal performance consultant since 2012. Prior to this, he spent 20 years as a national newspaper reporter, business presenter and PR consultant, building extensive experience in media, communications and strategic advisory roles.
He has lived in the unique and beautiful Garden Suburb since 2004 and is deeply committed to preserving its distinctive character and heritage.
William believes strongly in protecting and enhancing the Garden Suburb's unique qualities, ensuring residents receive excellent value-for-money public services, and preventing unnecessary council interference in local affairs. He is passionate about safeguarding the local environment and champions efficient, high-quality healthcare provision across both the NHS and private sector.

Leon  left school at tender age of 15 with zero qualifications but quickly built a memorable career. By 23 he was Marketing Director at BSA Group, managing a large support team. Left at 27 and founded an audio-visual production company, growing it and finally bought out by a publicly quoted international advertising group, later appointed as Deputy Chairman responsible for 8,000 employees across 45 countries. 
He worked with major global brands, overseeing PR and advertising at the highest level including the launch of political parties and radio stations including LBC and Capital Radio.

Married to Susan for 60 years, with children and grandchildren, Leon was fortunate to have experienced decades of leadership, commercial acumen and financial discipline. If elected, he would appreciate the opportunity to  apply my business experience to ensure Barnet Council delivers full value for money, strong oversight and professional management to his valued fellow residents.

I am standing at the May 7 Local elections in HGS. As an Independent Candidate. 
As many know for years I have been a de facto Councillor getting things done in HGS. And not just in the Rose Gardens! 
-I got the Lyttelton Cafe reopened after years of Barnet neglect. I got the Lyttelton Toilets fixed and repaired. There is now Soap and flushing. Even locks on the Cubicles; but still waiting for a Baby Changing Mat. 
-Behind the scenes I worked to get the Ossulton Way Zebra Crossing installed - fighting local Councillors and other local interests.
-Falloden way is a big deal  and my being elected will help me make this road safer.
-Ask me about the Lyttelton Path! And the proposed waterworks!
-I will donate my Councillors 'salary' (£15-25k) to the Community
Updated 8 April 2026.

candidates.stmt: Born and raised in Hendon and Golders Green, Gedale Weinberg is a proud lifelong member of the community he still calls home. At 56 and happily married, his values are grounded in family, integrity, and responsibility to future generations. He built a successful international career as a diamond dealer, working across Europe, the United States, and the UK. Operating in global markets sharpened his commercial judgement, negotiation skills, and understanding of international affairs. Inspired to enter public life after meeting Nigel Farage in Miami, Gedale is committed to delivering principled leadership locally and nationally.

Simon Tobi is a highly experienced property professional with over 15 years in senior property and housing management roles. Legally trained with a First-Class Law degree and a Master of Laws, he began his career in legal practice and mortgage advisory before moving into property management.
Simon has managed large residential and mixed-use portfolios across London and the UK, leading multidisciplinary teams and overseeing compliance, finance, and strategic operations.
He has extensive expertise in Section 20 legislation, service charge budgeting, health and safety, and regulatory liaison. Simon combines legal insight with commercial acumen to deliver efficient, compliant and results-driven property management leadership.

candidates.stmt: Jeremy Apfel has lived in Hendon for over 20 years and has two sons at local schools. He works in finance and believes council services are vital to local wellbeing and should deliver real social value. Active locally, he wants council tax spent wisely, waste cut, and services improved. Barnet’s failures in housing, children’s services and financial management are clear. Residents deserve safe, clean, pothole-free streets, efficient services, and a council focused on delivering the best outcomes for local people.

Hannah Messham is a Hendon resident and Head of Operations with a strong record of delivering results. Active within the local Jewish community, she believes Hendon deserves principled, common-sense leadership. Hannah supports Reform UK’s commitment to free speech, tough action on crime, smaller and more effective government, and secure borders. She wants to uphold Reform values locally, cut waste at Barnet Council, and ensure residents’ voices are properly heard.

Dylan Marshall lives in Hendon and has proudly served in the British Army for many years. As an instructor with the Army Cadet Force, he continues to mentor and support young people in our community. Dylan is standing to represent West Hendon with integrity, practical leadership, and a strong sense of public service — driven by his commitment to make our area safer, stronger, and better for everyone.

I am standing as the Labour candidate for Hendon and have lived in the borough of Barnet for 30 years.
I have a professional background in finance and have developed strong problem-solving skills and a deep understanding of economic challenges, which I want to use to support local residents and businesses.
I am actively involved in community work. I am a school governor at a Hendon school and also serve on community boards focused on improving neighbourhood safety and resident wellbeing.
Hendon is a vibrant and diverse community and I want to ensure it remains a place where residents feel safe, supported, and heard. My priorities include improving community safety and promoting a cleaner, healthier environment.
I am committed to listening to residents, representing your concerns and working hard to deliver solutions that make a real difference.

“I am delighted to be standing for Hendon as part of a full slate of Green Party candidates. My family live there and I still work in the area as a gardener so I walk the walk, not just talk the talk regarding net zero for climate and nature.”

candidates.stmt:
I am a lifelong Barnet resident who has been educated locally attending the local primary school, Dame Alice Owens, Barnet College and then Middlesex University studying business management. I have volunteered and worked across Barnet currently in the local hospital. Like many residents I have seen first-hand the decline of our high street and public services being pushed to the brink after years of Tory and Labour failure . Our local pubs and business are increasingly struggling to survive in the current political and economic environment.
I am standing to listen to local residents, our council should puts them first, keep our streets safe, balance the books and invest in public infrastructure. Barnet council should serve its residents not itself. If you want real change then vote for me in High Barnet.

I have spent my entire career working with money that matters to real people. I started out training in a small accountancy firm, then sharpened my skills at Deloitte, one of the world's leading professional services firms, before founding my own practice in 1990. For 35 years I have worked alongside small business owners, entrepreneurs and families, helping them manage their finances, grow their livelihoods and navigate a system that often feels stacked against them. That experience has taught me something important: when money is managed well, communities thrive. When it is not, ordinary people pay the price. Our high streets are under real pressure. Local businesses are struggling with rising costs, footfall is falling and too many shopfronts stand empty. Public services feel stretched, and residents too often feel their concerns go unheard at council level. I have seen this first-hand through my clients and through my years as a community advocate in this area. I am standing as your Reform Party councillor because I believe our council deserves the same financial discipline and transparency that any well-run business relies on. I will bring 35 years of professional expertise directly to bear on how public money is spent, where savings can be made without cutting the services residents depend on, and how we hold decision-makers properly to account. This is not about politics for its own sake. It is about getting the basics right: a safer neighbourhood, a stronger local economy and a council that works for you.

WE WILL DO EVERYTHING WE CAN TO STOP THE 11-STOREY DEVELOPMENT AT HIGH BARNET TUBE STATION

About me:

I was born in Enfield Chase Hospital, and moved to High Barnet 15 years ago.  My youngest daughter was born in Barnet Hospital. Both my children went to Christ Church Primary School.

I was a solicitor in the City for 10 years. Now, I run a fintech business from High Barnet.

Last year the doctors and nurses at Barnet Hospital saved my life. As I recovered I wanted to give back to my local community.  I renewed my Lib Dem membership and I currently volunteer at Barnet Environmental Centre.  You may have seen me litter-picking around the library from time to time.

Every great journey begins with a single step...

WE WILL DO EVERYTHING WE CAN TO STOP THE 11-STOREY DEVELOPMENT AT HIGH BARNET TUBE STATION

About me:

Born in New Zealand, I moved to the UK in 1998 and to High Barnet more than 13 years ago. My two children attend Christ Church Primary and QEGS, with my son soon to start at the Ark. I have been serving as a parent trustee at QEGS for two years.

I work for a consultancy specialising in tech solutions for the public sector. In 2022 I led the launch of the Homes for Ukraine scheme which matched refugees in urgent need with host families in the UK.

We're lucky to live here and it would be a privilege to have the opportunity to represent you.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/mill-hill (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
Most candidates will tell you what they might do if elected. I would rather tell you what I have already been doing for the past seven years.

Seven years ago, I became Chair of the Mill Hill Residents’ Association. What began as a simple wish for my family and children to feel part of a real community where people know and support each other quickly became a personal passion. Over time, that grew into a deep sense of responsibility and loyalty to the people of Mill Hill.

Over the past seven years, I have worked day in and day out to grow the Residents’ Association from around 100 members to more than 2,200, supported by a large committee and over 150 active volunteers. I have brought people together, built local initiatives, and stood up for residents giving them a strong voice when no one else would.

You have seen how hard I have worked, how strongly I have fought for you, and what I have achieved for Mill Hill.

I have done all of this with no political backing. Now imagine how much more could be achieved with the tools and authority of an elected councillor.

That is why I am standing as an INDEPENDENT candidate free from party control, party whips, and party agendas and focused 100% on residents and the community of Mill Hill.

As your independent councillor, I will continue to build community, stand up for residents, and ensure you have a real voice in local decisions.

I am standing alongside Danny Al-Khafaji, a committed and active MHRA committee member. Together, as a team, we will be able to achieve even more for you.

On May 7th, you have three votes to choose three councillors for Mill Hill. We would be honoured if you trusted us with two of them (one for me, Frank Orman, and one for Danny Al-Khafaji) so that together, we can go further, move faster, and do even more for you, your family, and our community.

I am a respected jeweller and gemmologist with over 25 years' experience in the UK jewellery industry. i am  a Mill Hill resident for more than 24 years, i raised four children locally with my wife Sophie.

I believes Britain is ready for the Reform UK approach in these challenging times. Familiar with Mill Hill's key issues—traffic, parking, parks, local businesses, education, youth activities and crime, i want to be elected to use my creativity and experience to give residents a strong, practical voice.

Vote Reform UK to improve Mill Hill.

am a young Reform UK activist for Mill Hill. I wasn’t part of the system that allowed wasteful spending to become normal, and I think too many of the real issues people deal with every day have been ignored for too long.

People are paying high council tax, yet still dealing with basic problems like potholes that don’t get fixed, concerns about local crime, and services that don’t feel good enough for what they cost. It often feels like residents are being asked to pay more while getting less in return.

I believe local politics should focus on the issues people actually notice day to day, not just what’s coming out of council offices. Residents deserve councillors who are visible, accountable, and focused on everyday problems, rather than getting lost in bureaucracy or distracted by things that don’t make a real difference.

Most candidates will tell you what they believe. I would rather tell you what I have been doing and who I am doing it for.

Mill Hill deserves better than to be an afterthought in someone else's national agenda. Political parties fight elections here, but their loyalty is elsewhere - to their leadership, their whips, their party line. Mine is to **you**.

I'm standing as an **independent** candidate alongside my neighbour Frank Orman. Not as a protest, but as a proposition: that this ward is best served by people with no master but the residents who live here.

When I put down roots in Mill Hill, I made a decision: to get involved, not just to settle in. Joining the Mill Hill Residents' Association committee was the natural next step, and it has changed how I see this place. Working alongside Frank Orman for the last few years, week in and week out, for over 2,000 members who care deeply about this community, I stopped thinking of Mill Hill as somewhere I lived and started thinking of it as somewhere I was responsible for.

That sense of responsibility is why I am standing. Frank and I are not career politicians. We have no party telling us what to think or who to answer to. We are two local leaders who have already been actively working for Mill Hill - and who want to do more.

I would be honoured to have your support.

As an educator and trade unionist, I believe that we are better off if we work together, learn more about each other, and make decisions together about our collective futures.

We deserve better choices than the same old divisive two-party politics of hate, fear and austerity. Too often decisions are made about us, without us; for the benefit of the wealthy few, not for the many.

If elected I intend to put the local community at the heart of my decision making, fighting for more local services, affordable housing, and real social justice. So vote Green at these council elections.

Vote for Hope not Hate.

I am standing in Mill Hill for Reform UK, on May 7th 2026. After a long career at Export Credits Guarantee Department, I served as a Barnet Councillor 2010 to 2018, and I hope to be re elected to serve the residents of Mill Hill again

Vote Reform UK to improve Mill Hill.
