candidates.stmt:
Adam Collins is a Reform UK candidate with over 20 years experience delivering digital transformation and customer-focused solutions across financial services, travel, property and construction, alongside leading major projects as a professional property manager.
A Barnet resident for more than 15 years, he has seen rising violent crime, county-lines drug activity, antisemitism and declining local services in Totteridge & Woodside. Adam believes Labour and Conservative mismanagement has failed residents. He supports Reform UK's plan to restore safety, fiscal responsibility, cleaner streets and accountable local government

With over 30 years' service as a Metropolitan Police Officer, Richard held senior Federation roles in armed policing, counter-terrorism and was the Federation Lead for Royalty & Specialist Protection,

In 2024 he was voted for by his peers to become the Chair of the Metropolitan Police Federation, representing over 30,000 Metropolitan Police Officers, and was widely respected for his fearless representation, never hesitating to challenge wrongdoing or speak truth to power. He is passionate about restoring law & order, and tackling crime blighting London's streets, where offenders too often act with impunity.
A long term local resident, Richard has served his community through voluntary roles for over 20 years. He brings unmatched frontline experience and leadership to local government and is ready to make a real difference in Barnet.

A lifelong Totteridge resident and a Business Management student & University Ambassador. Katie has gained professional experience with Eve Johnson Racing, building strong skills in teamwork and organisation.
Katie has volunteered with Starlight Children's Charity and the Queen's Green Canopy, showing her commitment to community and the environment. She has seen first hand, the decline in local services and the impact this has had on residents and prosperity in the area. She believes Barnet urgently needs fresh thinking and restored trust in local government to protect the future for this and next generation

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/underhill (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
From reversing Tory neglect of our green spaces, to pioneering institutional change in mental health support, I’ve always shown that no matter how big the challenge, I’ll always take it on and get the job done.

Having gone to school at Yavneh College in Borehamwood, worked my first job at a retailer in the Boulevard, and grown up across the constituency, I’ve seen how our community supports one another.

But the Conservatives have driven our country into the ground - and they’ve taken voters in Hertsmere for granted for too long. I saw that firsthand when knocking on doors during last year’s council elections, which showed us that people here are ready for change.

We need a transformative Labour government to pick us back up. Oliver Dowden doesn’t fight for the change we need - he’s part of the problem, at the centre of successive failed Tory governments.

But we can only put that right with a Labour government that offers a fresh start - and our communities must be able to trust Labour in government once again.

My great-great grandfather, fleeing persecution in eastern Europe, opened a kosher restaurant in the East-End, passed down through generations. My dad worked there night and day to make sure our family had a roof over our head. It was at the centre of my family life, and grounded me in my local Jewish community.

I’m from this community - it taught me to work hard, stand up for others, give back and fight for what you believe in. It’s why I joined the Civil Service, volunteer for cancer care charities and food banks, and why I am a trustee for a homelessness charity. And it’s why I campaign for Labour, day in, day out.

We need to make hope a reality for so many in Hertsmere. Our education system is too rigid, focused on exams instead of creativity and skills for life. Too many in society are left too long without adequate mental health support. We need to tackle the cost of living crisis, fix our rundown public services, and embrace the opportunities of

Andrew has lived in Barnet for over 40 years after moving from Devon. He spent more than three decades as an Immigration Officer at Heathrow and Luton Airports, working on the frontline of Britain’s border security and public safety.

Having seen first-hand how both Labour and the Conservatives failed to control immigration, Andrew believes only Reform UK has the strength and common sense to restore border integrity and rebuild trust.

In Underhill, Andrew has campaigned alongside residents against over-development at High Barnet Underground Station and has been a strong voice against ULEZ expansion and damaging traffic schemes.

Now retired, he splits his time between restoring classic cars, doting on his Grandsons, and serving the community which he’s passionate about and been a part of for four decades. Andrew wants to stand as he has seen the decline occurring over the years in his community. He is eager to make Barnet much safer, the Council well managed and effectively run and see the local residents have a valid voice which is finally listened to.

Alison has lived in Barnet for 18 years and brings a wealth of public-service experience.

Her background spans education and local government finance, alongside decades of service working for the public.

She has also managed mixed property portfolios, overseeing repairs and refurbishments, and working closely with tenants, owners, and contractors — giving her a strong understanding of housing issues from the ground up.

Locally, Alison has consistently campaigned for better housing and infrastructure, the protection of local communities, and against unnecessary council spending on unwanted road and infrastructure changes.

She is determined to help improve Barnet for all residents and is committed to giving local people a stronger voice — one that has been ignored for far too long.

I’ve lived in Barnet all my life and in Underhill for over 24 years, so I know the area - and the issues - extremely well. Over that time, I’ve worked closely with local residents, representing their concerns and helping to drive real change in our community.

In 2010, working alongside residents, I led a successful five-year campaign to protect local public open space from development, securing its registration as a Village Green following a public inquiry. Since then, I’ve continued to protect our green spaces and am now working to bring the long-derelict former Quinta Club building back into community use.

More recently, I’ve worked with residents to challenge unfair parking schemes, including Controlled Parking Zones introduced without clear evidence, and excessive parking charges at local shops that are harming small businesses. Through sustained pressure, we have already reduced the scale of one scheme and continue to push for fairer, evidence-based solutions.

I’ve also supported residents affected by stalled regeneration at Dollis Valley, worked with Barnet Hospital and the Council on parking pressures, and consistently challenged inappropriate development - particularly on Green Belt land or where it fails to reflect the character of the area. I support more genuinely affordable housing, but it must be in the right places and designed properly.

I have also represented residents for years on the local Community Action Panel, working with police and councillors to help set neighbourhood policing priorities, including tackling burglary, vehicle crime and antisocial behaviour.

I currently serve as Chair of the Quinta Village Green Residents’ Association and previously served as a school governor at Whitings Hill Primary School, including five years as Chair. I am also a local parent and run a family business in the renewable energy sector.

I’m standing as an Independent because I believe local decisions should be driven by what’s right for Underhill - not b

Sean has lived in Barnet since he was a child. He lives in East Barnet and has stood in the ward twice before. A former Barnet Councillor he has also stood for Chipping Barnet for Parliament three times in late 90s and early 2000s 

He works as head of a Government approved  redress organisation, The Property Redress Scheme,  resolving complaints made against letting and estate agents. 

His primary focus for the area is to ensure that the development of the Victoria Quarter proposed for the old gas works site is done in a sensitive, sustainable and environmentally friendly way. 

“The plans put forward before were unacceptable with far too many dwellings and no solution for the impact it will have on the local area. Yes we need more homes and affordable one at that however the need is for family homes not densely packed high rise flats with no consideration for quality of life”

He wants better transport local links especially since hundreds of residents lost access to their bus route when the 384 was rerouted. “I will lobby for a new bus route to restore the access lost, which would connect those most in need to local shops and New Barnet station. It could also serve the new development if we can get a sensible scheme built.”

Local crime has escalated in the area and Sean will campaign for more police to deal with the rise in burglary and anti social behaviour. 

“When I was a councillor we had local neighbourhood wardens and community policing, we need to restore the sense of security and trust in the area, which is being eroded by the lack of enforcement.” 

Sean believes that local politicians need to get back closer to their communities. “Especially after this difficult year, residents need to see active, local champions, standing up for them and what they want to see for their area”

I am a 20-year-old student, grassroots organiser, and your Trade Unionist and Socialist Candidate. I am not a career politician, but someone who has grown up here in Barnet and attended school at Queen Elizabeth’s. For too long, the services we own and cherish have been sold off to rich companies. Our money and land has been stolen to build expensive houses for wealthy landlords, while residents are forced to pay the price via tax grabs and outrageous parking fees. I am standing to fight to reclaim Barnet for residents.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/west-finchley (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place, spoilt ballots include ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt: Barnet deserves better than broken promises. The two-party system has failed on the cost of living and the climate crisis. I’m here to listen to residents and work for a fairer future that protects our communities and the planet.

Danny Feldman is a common-sense activist for West Finchley.
A Finchley resident for 12 years with three children in local schools, he understands the challenges facing local families. With over 20 years of running his own business, he brings real commercial experience and a focus on results.
His priorities are spending public money wisely, keeping streets safer, and revitalising local high streets. Danny believes in practical, fact-based decisions that deliver real improvements for residents.

Anirudh Shah is a long-standing Barnet resident and qualified Gas Engineer who has lived in the borough since moving to the UK in 1990.
He is a graduate in pharmaceutical science from North London University and has witnessed profound changes affecting everyday life for ordinary people.
Motivated to stand up for his community, Anirudh wants to bring people together to improve local high streets, support young people and voluntary groups, and tackle the rising costs of energy, food, and housing. He is committed to practical community action and active local involvement.

>My Priorities for a Safer, Cleaner City:

Zero Tolerance on Financial Crime: I will push for a rigorous investigation into money laundering within our city. We must ensure that local funds are never used to silence activists or support criminal elements.

Ending the "Pothole Crisis": Our roads are a safety hazard. I will prioritize fixing our infrastructure over bloated consultancy fees.

Total Transparency: No more "hiding" the budget. I will fight to expose where your money goes and stop the funding of agendas that make Barnet an unsafe area.

>Where Your Money Is Going vs. Where It Should Go:

The current budget shows a massive misallocation of your Council Tax. I will fight to redirect these funds:

Agency & Consultants (£1.6m every month): Could be spent on permanent local staff and street cleaning.

Senior Management (£100k+ salaries): Could be used to protect vital advice services currently at risk of closure.

Developer Subsidies (£41m CIL funds): Should be invested in local schools, parks, and health clinics.

Debt Interest (Thousands daily): Reducing this debt is the only way to prevent the 4.98% Council Tax hikes.

Barnet is becoming an "Unsafe Spending Area" due to these choices. I am committed to following the money, exposing the waste, and putting the residents of Barnet first.

On election day, vote for transparency. Vote for Ali Haji

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/west-hendon (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt: I am standing in West Hendon to improve the area, which has been neglected for years. I have been a Councillor since 2010, and helped hundreds of residents over 16 years. if elected I will improve West Hendon visually, ensure that all potholes are fixed and bring permanent solution to fly tipping issues. We will focus on rejuvenation of the high streets by parking incentives

I am standing in West Hendon to improve the area and restore basic standards that have been allowed to slip.

I run a local property staging and interior design business, and having lived in Barnet for over 20 years, I have seen standards decline - with residents paying higher taxes but getting less in return

If elected, my focus will be on practical improvements that make a visible difference - tackling fly tipping through enforcement and prevention, improving street cleaning, and ensuring potholes are properly fixed.

I will push for a stronger community safety presence so residents feel safer and anti-social behaviour is dealt with early, not ignored. I will support sensible measures to strengthen our high streets, including short-stay free parking to make it easier for residents to shop locally.

I am determined to make Barnet cleaner, safer and better managed.

I am standing for Barnet in West Hendon, I hope to improve the area significantly if elected and will always be available to residents any time of the day. I am currently a social worker for a large charity and I love giving back to residents and will help them with any issues

I’ve felt so welcomed by the West Hendon community since I moved here 7 years ago. I’ve met friends and neighbours by attending litter picks, co-founding a knitting group, helping to put on several Summer Festivals, doing conservation work at the Welsh Harp, and starting work on a community garden.

It would be a great honour to represent you all as your local councillor, to leverage council support for local initiatives, and to help solve issues that we’re facing as a neighbourhood.

wards.results_url: https://www.barnet.gov.uk/elections-and-voting/local-government-elections-thursday-7-may-2026/results/whetstone (turnout percentage correct to one decimal place)

candidates.stmt:
“I grew up in Barnet and feel privileged to be bringing up my own children here as well.

Through volunteering at a nursing home, foodbank and synagogues in Barnet, I have spent my life trying to improve things for our borough – which is why I’m standing to be your Labour Councillor for Whetstone.”

Kevin is a resident of Whetstone and is deeply committed to our local area. He has worked in the dental industry in marketing and practice management, gaining experience in clinics across London.

A dedicated member of the community, Kevin regularly engages with local groups, taking part in litter-picking initiatives and volunteering at local events.

Living in Whetstone, he understands the importance of ensuring that residents have a say in decisions that affect their area. His main priority is to connect with locals and make sure that what happens in the community reflects their needs.

Over the years, Kevin has campaigned across Whetstone and looks forward to continuing this work, meeting residents over the coming months in the run-up to the election.

I’m Adrian Kitching your local Reform UK candidate for the Whetstone election on 7th May. I live just down the road in Totteridge, where I’m a proud parent of a child attending school in Barnet, and the owner of Marley, our beloved Pets As Therapy dog, who brings joy and comfort to people in the community.

After over 30 years in business, leading UK and international projects in sales, marketing and manufacturing, I’ve seen first-hand what makes communities thrive and what holds them back. As Managing Director of Blue Cow Limited, I’ve worked closely with small businesses and entrepreneurs, helping them grow, create jobs, and navigate the ever-changing landscape of government regulation and economic pressure.

But I’ve also grown increasingly frustrated, like many of you with politicians who are out of touch, more focused on party lines than real lives. It’s time for change.

I’m standing for Reform UK because I believe passionately in the principles of family, community, and country. These aren’t just slogans, they’re the foundation of a strong, stable and successful Britain.

✔ I will fight for lower taxes, sensible spending, and real support for working families
✔ I will stand up for small businesses, local services and British industries 
✔ I support common-sense immigration control and a return to pride in British values
✔ I believe in free speech, fairness, and putting Whetstone residents first

Let’s bring common sense back to politics

Vote Adrian Kitching – Reform UK – Whetstone By-Election

Martin is a Whetstone resident for over 20 years who knows the area inside out. A specialist in countering financial crime, he has worked on anti-money laundering and sanctions compliance, advising governmental and non-governmental institutions in the UK and overseas.
A published author and media commentator, Martin is not a career politician and is standing to restore public safety, tackle anti-social behaviour, protect local services and help fix Barnet Council's finances – putting residents first.

Having lived in Chipping Barnet for 24 years now, I am frustrated there is no-one else I can vote for in our constituency prepared to address the elephant in the room head on. Brexit is destroying jobs, fuelling the cost-of-living crisis and making travel harder for all of us. It must be reversed.

candidates.stmt:
Ishpal Anand has lived in Finchley for almost fifty years. Having been raised, educated, and now raising his own family in the Woodhouse and North Finchley community, he has deep local knowledge of the area. He is concerned by recent increases in fly-tipping, anti-social behaviour, car crime and shoplifting.
With over 25 years in the IT industry, including 18 years as a Business Analyst, Ishpal brings strong problem-solving skills and is committed to keeping Woodhouse safe, clean and thriving for future generation

Sanghera - A London-based operations manager in the chauffeur and private transport industry, Parveen believes Britain works best when standards are high, rules are clear, and responsibility matters. She values professionalism, order, and competence, especially in a capital that depends on them every day. Proud of Britain's history, institutions, and moral foundations, she believes national identity should be understood and protected, and wants Britain presented as confident, well-run, and respectful of its heritage, particularly through tourism.
